title: Discovery Service: Full Blockchain Integration (Status Anchoring, Registry Verification, Chain Indexer)
description: Connect the Discovery Service to the deployed on-chain contracts (IssuerRegistry, TrustPolicyRegistry, StatusRegistry, ServiceRegistry) via the shared chain client library (TASK-020). Implement: policy/issuer cache with TTL, status list hash anchoring after each publish, chain event indexing, and admin endpoints for chain health and event browsing.
objectives: Discovery always has fresh knowledge of on-chain issuer/policy state via TTL cache with warm reload
Every status list publish triggers a best-effort on-chain anchor (StatusRegistry.updateStatus)
Admin operators can inspect chain state, event lag, and recent contract events from the Discovery UI

steps: Step 1 - Integration Point: Import ChainClient and the contract client classes from libs/chain (TASK-020). In DiscoverySettings: blockchain_rpc_url, chain_id, contract_addresses (issuer_registry, trust_policy_registry, status_registry, service_registry), anchor_status_lists (bool, default false in dev, true in prod), chain_rpc_timeout_seconds (default 10).

Step 2 - Chain Health Check at Startup: In lifespan startup: call ChainClient.get_block_number(). If the call fails AND chain_required_at_startup=true → fail startup with clear error. If chain_required_at_startup=false (dev default) → log warning and continue with chain_available=false flag. Set a global circuit breaker for chain operations.

Step 3 - ChainPolicyCache: Create services/discovery/src/discovery/services/chain_policy_cache.py. At startup and on TTL expiry (default 300s): read IssuerRegistryClient.get_all_issuers() → list of (did, is_active, schema_hashes). Read TrustPolicyRegistryClient.get_policies(env) → policy parameters per env. Store in an in-process dict with last_loaded_at timestamp. Expose is_issuer_active(did) → bool and get_policy(env) → PolicyParams. On cache miss: trigger async reload. Falls back to stale cache if chain RPC fails (logs warning, emits metric chain_cache_stale=1).

Step 4 - Status Anchor Hook: In StatusListService.publish() (TASK-030), after writing to DB: if ANCHOR_STATUS_LIST_ON_CHAIN and chain_available: call StatusRegistryClient.anchor_status_list(status_list_id, current_hash) in a background task (fire-and-forget with retry). Do NOT await this in the hot revocation path — revocation is effective immediately locally. On success: update status_lists.anchor_tx_hash and anchor_pending=false. On failure: set anchor_pending=true, retry via background worker (exponential backoff, max 5 attempts).

Step 5 - Anchor Retry Worker: Background task (Arq queue). Worker task anchor_pending_status_lists(): SELECT * FROM status_lists WHERE anchor_pending=true AND anchor_attempts < 5 AND next_retry_at < now(). For each: attempt on-chain anchor. On success: clear anchor_pending, store tx_hash. On failure: increment anchor_attempts, set next_retry_at = now() + 2^attempts * 30s (exponential backoff).

Step 6 - ServiceRegistry Integration: In ServiceCRUD.create() (TASK-024): if REGISTER_SERVICE_ON_CHAIN: after DB insert, call ServiceRegistryClient.register_service(service_id, owner_did, env, schema_hash) in background task. Store tx_hash in services table. If chain fails: set chain_sync_pending=true, retry worker picks up.

Step 7 - Chain Events Indexer: Create services/discovery/src/discovery/services/chain_indexer.py. Background polling worker (every 30s): call ChainClient.get_logs(from_block, to_block) for each contract. Parse events: IssuerRegistered, IssuerDisabled, PolicyUpdated, ServiceRegistered, StatusAnchorUpdated. Upsert into chain_events table: { tx_hash, block_number, event_name, contract, args_json, indexed_at }. Update last_indexed_block in Discovery state table.

Step 8 - Chain Admin Endpoints: GET /api/v1/chain/status: requires viewer role. Returns { network, chain_id, rpc_url (masked), latest_block, indexer_last_block, indexer_lag (blocks), is_available, contracts: { issuer_registry: { address, checked_at, is_responding }, ... } }. GET /api/v1/chain/events: requires viewer role. Query params: contract, event_name, from_block, since (ISO-8601), limit (max 100). Returns paginated list of indexed events.

Step 9 - Metrics: Emit Prometheus metrics: chain_rpc_request_duration_seconds (histogram, labels: method), chain_rpc_errors_total (counter, label: method), chain_indexer_lag_blocks (gauge), chain_anchor_pending_count (gauge), chain_policy_cache_age_seconds (gauge).

Step 10 - Chain Outage Handling: If ChainClient throws RpcError: log structured error, increment chain_rpc_errors_total metric, check circuit breaker state. If circuit OPEN: skip chain operation, use cached data, log 'chain_circuit_open'. If circuit closed and error threshold exceeded: trip circuit (OPEN for 60s, then HALF-OPEN test). All chain operations wrapped in try/except with graceful degradation — chain errors NEVER cause 500s on non-chain endpoints.

error_handling.message: Chain RPC connection failed. Cannot start Discovery Service.

error_handling.behavior: Circuit breaker trips, cached data used, chain_circuit_open metric=1 — no 500 returned to callers
Log critical alert, set anchor_failed=true in DB, emit chain_anchor_failed metric. Manual operator action required.

security_constraints: Chain RPC credentials (API key, private signing key) loaded from SecretStorage — never from plain env vars in prod
chain_events table is append-only — no DELETE or UPDATE at application layer
Admin chain endpoints require viewer/operator role — chain state is read-only for operators
Circuit breaker prevents cascade failures when chain is unstable — fail-safe degraded mode

acceptance_criteria: At startup, chain contracts validate successfully (or Discovery starts in degraded mode with warning)
After POST /revoke, a status anchor transaction appears in status_lists.anchor_tx_hash within 5 minutes
ChainPolicyCache refreshes correctly — is_issuer_active() returns updated state after issuer is disabled on-chain
With chain RPC down: all non-chain endpoints continue operating normally; chain_circuit_open metric = 1
